articles.title: FASTER DELIVERY
articles.summary: In this recorded session [4], senior engineers from Red Hat and Sony Interactive Entertainment explained how they use Miro's collaboration tools to transform engineering productivity [4].

articles.title: ELIMINATING JAVASCRIPT COLD STARTS ON AWS LAMBDA
articles.summary: Porffor is a new JavaScript engine that compiles JS code ahead-of-time to WebAssembly and native binaries, creating much smaller and faster executables compared to Node.js and other runtimes. In AWS Lambda cold start benchmarks, Porffor performed ~12x faster than Node.js and ~4x faster than Amazon's LLRT while being over 2x cheaper to run than Node.js. However, Porffor is still in pre-alpha development with limited JavaScript support and no current Node.js API compatibility, making it unsuitable for production use yet.

articles.title: FROM PYTHON TO GO: WHY WE REWROTE OUR INGEST PIPELINE AT TELEMETRY HARBOR
articles.summary: At Telemetry Harbor, the team initially chose Python FastAPI for their time-series data ingestion pipeline to prioritize rapid prototyping and market validation, but as their user base grew, they encountered severe performance bottlenecks with CPU usage spiking to 800% and system crashes under moderate loads. After careful evaluation between Rust and Go, they decided to rewrite their core ingest system in Go due to its balance of performance, simplicity, and easier learning curve compared to Rust's steep complexity.

articles.title: FLASHLIST V2: A GROUND-UP REWRITE FOR REACT NATIVE'S NEW ARCHITECTURE
articles.summary: Shopify first released FlashList in 2022 and has now introduced FlashList v2, a complete rewrite of React Native's list component that eliminates the need for devs to provide item size estimates and achieves better load and scroll perf by using React Native's New Architecture for synchronous layout measurements. FlashList v2 is now production-ready and powers thousands of lists in Shopify's mobile app, with 2M+ monthly downloads.

articles.title: BEST PRACTICES FOR BUILDING AGENTIC AI SYSTEMS: WHAT ACTUALLY WORKS IN PRODUCTION
articles.summary: This article outlines best practices for building agentic AI systems based on real production experience. The optimal architecture features a two-tier system with Primary Agents that maintain context and orchestrate tasks, plus stateless Subagents that work like pure functions without memory or state. Critical principles include keeping subagents stateless for parallel execution, using structured communication protocols, and employing orchestration patterns like MapReduce and Sequential Pipeline, depending on task dependencies.

articles.title: HOW TO SCALE CODE REVIEWS
articles.summary: Key practices to scaling code reviews include keeping changes small, responding to reviews within 24 hours, and using automated checks like unit tests and static analysis to let reviewers focus on high-level feedback. This article advocates for a structured 5-step workflow from PR creation through deployment, complemented by AI tools to reduce review time and improve developer velocity.

articles.title: MINECRAFT MCP SERVER
articles.summary: Microsoft has released a Minecraft MCP server that enables Claude and other supported LLMs to control a Minecraft character.

articles.title: CRONICLE
articles.summary: Cronicle is a multi-server task scheduler and runner with a web-based front-end UI that handles both scheduled, repeating, and on-demand jobs across multiple slave servers. It provides real-time statistics and a live log viewer for monitoring job execution.

articles.title: THE FUTURE OF JAVASCRIPT: WHAT AWAITS US
articles.summary: JavaScript continues to evolve rapidly. The TC39 committee has advanced nine proposals, including the newly standardized explicit resource management feature using the using keyword for automatic cleanup, and Array.fromAsync for handling async iterables. Other notable developments include candidate features like immutable ArrayBuffers, draft proposals for deterministic random number generation and number clamping methods, and early-stage proposals for improved internationalization formatting and comparison utilities.

articles.title: REACT INTERNALS: WHICH USEEFFECT HOOK RUNS FIRST
REACT-IS-AWFUL
articles.summary: An AI generated book about React that teaches React and humorously complains about its complexity and design decisions.

articles.title: FIXING A 10 YEAR OLD MISTAKE IN RAILS (PULL REQUEST)
articles.summary: This PR found and removed a 10 year old mistake in Rails, proving that there's always low-hanging performance-gaining fruit to be found.
